summary:
Neither excessive maternal size nor fetal smallness confers an advantage, as both are tied to severe complications — macrosomia leading to difficult births and fetal growth restriction causing poor newborn outcomes. Balanced nutrition and physiological harmony, not body size, determine favorable results, supporting the conclusion that being “big” has no clear benefit.  

\boxed{C}

\boxed{C}. Answer choice: C
